title: Lastrea weberi
intro: There is limited information about the plant Lastrea weberi.
uses: Lastrea weberi has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help in treating wounds and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi. However, the effectiveness and safety of using Lastrea weberi for medicinal purposes have not been extensively scientifically studied. Further research is needed to confirm its potential benefits and potential side effects.

parts: Lastrea weberi, also known as the Weber's fern, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal parts of Lastrea weberi are the leaves, which are traditionally used in local medicine to treat various health issues. The leaves contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to their medicinal value. In traditional medicine, Lastrea weberi is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal properties and uses of Lastrea weberi.
